Missed DDA's booking deadline? Buyers for Karkardooma property get another extension
description: The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) has extended the booking period for its 'DDA Towering Heights' scheme in Karkardooma until June 30. This offers a renewed opportunity for homebuyers to secure flats in Delhi's tallest residential tower, a 48-storey project developed under the Transit Oriented Development policy.

title: UNSC membership huge responsibility, not forum for peddling biased, false narratives: India slams Pakistan
description: Pakistan, which constantly raises the issue of Jammu and Kashmir as well as other topics internal to India at various UN platforms, currently sits in the Security Council as a non-permanent member and its tenure will end this year

description: Attorney Yaakov Roth, speaking during an exchange with U.S. Appeals Court Judge Patricia Millett, said only Congress could halt the $400 million project.

title: Federal judge strikes down Trump administration immigration policy affecting 39 countries
description: U.S. District Chief Judge said the policy “threw the lives of countless immigrants living in the United States into indeterminate legal limbo”, and he accused the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services of ignoring the law

title: Huddle - Mind Games: Mentalist leaves audience spellbound
description: After serious and critical dialogues the whole day, this playful session comes as a welcome change.

description: The US Vice President went on to argue that the circumstances surrounding the case reflected broader societal failures and urged what he called "righteous anger" in response. Nowak was stabbed by 23-year-old Vickrum Digwa, who was convicted of murder this week and sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 21 years.

title: Under PM Modi, India’s economy resilient and showing sustained growth: BJP chief
description: The GDP growth rate of 7.7% in FY 2025-26 and 7.8% in Q4 of FY 2025-26 reflects robust economic activity and broad-based growth across sectors, says BJP President Nitin Nabin

description: Kuwait says it’s responding to ‘hostile’ missile and drone attacks; U.S. military says it shot down Iranian drones launched toward Strait of Hormuz

title: IIT-Roorkee clarifies admission process for students below eligibility marks
description: Under the current eligibility criteria, candidates seeking admission to IITs through JEE (Advanced) must either secure at least 75% marks in Class 12 or equivalent examinations.

title: 'If they are arrested': Sonam Wangchuk warns of six-week fast ahead of CJP protest
description: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk will begin a 42-day hunger strike if members of the Cockroach Janta Party are arrested ahead of their Jantar Mantar protest. Wangchuk, traveling from Ladakh, urged supporters to maintain a peaceful demonstration, warning against provocations that could tarnish the youth-led movement. The protest seeks the education minister's resignation over examination irregularities.

title: Pocket-friendly packets: Why Indians are swapping big snack packs for smaller ones
description: Consumers are increasingly opting for smaller pack sizes of everyday goods, from edible oils to soaps, as rising costs strain monthly budgets. This shift, driven by economic stress and geopolitical factors, is leading FMCG companies to reduce grammage in popular low-priced packs to maintain affordability and protect profit margins.

title: Peddi Box Office Collection Day 2 Live Update: Ram Charan film earns over Rs 150 crore
Sunil Gavaskar pauses speech mid-way to answer wife’s phone call
Signals from the sea: why sardines, jellyfish are washing ashore
description: Researchers at INCOIS Hyderabad say the findings underscore growing influence of climate variability on fish behaviour, coastal biodiversity and livelihoods of fishing communities, highlighting the need for advanced monitoring and early-warning systems

title: A delayed monsoon, an emerging El Nino and the long shadow of India’s Great Famine
description: Nearly 150 years ago, a prolonged monsoon failure triggered one of India’s worst famines which claimed at least 55 lakh lives. As scientists track changing ocean-atmosphere conditions, an uneasy question lingers: will the Super El Nino replay the script of the 1876-78 tragedy?

title: Bobby Deol’s Bandar remains captive to its own parochial provocations

title: Suryakumar Yadav: The entertainer who lost the script
Major T20 reset on cards: Shreyas Iyer set for top job, Sooryavanshi in line for debut
description: Indian cricket is poised for a significant shift as the selection committee meets to pick squads for upcoming T20I tours. Shreyas Iyer is reportedly set to become the new T20 captain, replacing Suryakumar Yadav, whose form has dipped. The selectors are also considering a maiden call-up for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

title: Morning Digest: Praggnanandhaa beats Keymer to win Norway Chess title; U.S. attacks Iranian coastal sites after Iran launches drones in latest flare-up, and more
description: Here is a select list of stories to start the day.

title: 'J&K was, is and will remain part of India': New Delhi rebukes Pakistan at UN
description: At the UNGA, India took a bold stand against Pakistan, critiquing its manipulation of UN discussions for political leverage. The Indian envoy reaffirmed that Jammu and Kashmir is a core component of India, calling out Pakistan's attempts to distort facts as flimsy and ineffective.

title: ‘Albania is not for sale’, protesters say over Kushner-linked luxury resort near a protected wetland
description: Environmentalists ‌oppose the project, which they say would affect several hundred hectares of ‌pristine ⁠beaches and thousands of flamingos that nest and pass through nearby areas each ⁠year.

title: Curing India of a disease called capital-itis
Gambhir lays out plan to resurrect India's Test fortunes over the next nine months
description: Ahead of the Afghanistan Test, coach Gautam Gambhir stressed the importance of this match for the World Test Championship roadmap.  He highlighted the need to identify a fourth spinner for the Sri Lanka tour and expressed confidence in Sai Sudharsan's potential at number three, emphasizing a long-term approach to player development.

title: A neighbourhood called Hauz Rani
'Let 2 Bangladeshis stay for money': Delhi cops probe Malviya Nagar hotel owner for trafficking
description: Delhi Police is investigating Lovkesh Bajaj, linked to a deadly Hauz Rani fire, for suspected involvement in an international human trafficking racket. He allegedly used medical tourism as a front to smuggle foreigners into India, having previously been arrested for facilitating forged documents for a Bangladeshi family.

Why is Trump pushing faster use of AI in the military, and what are the safeguards?
CBSE extends post-result application deadline after students flag portal issues
description: CBSE has extended the deadline for Class 12 answer sheet verification and re-evaluation applications to June 7 due to portal access issues. This decision offers students more time to address concerns about scanned copies and evaluation discrepancies. The board previously opened its portal for students to report issues like missing pages or incorrect answer sheets.

title: Putin rules out meeting Zelenskyy and vows to pursue war goals
description: The Russia President said he saw “no point” in meeting Mr. Zelenskyy until a possible peace deal had been agreed; the Ukrainian President hit back, saying the Russian leader was “weak” and “choosing war again”

title: Praggnanandhaa becomes first Indian to raid Carlsen's backyard, win Norway Chess title
description: Rameshbabu Praggnanandhaa has achieved a historic victory at Norway Chess, becoming the first Indian to win the prestigious tournament. He secured the title by defeating Vincent Keymer in a classical game, with a draw between Wesley So and Alireza Firouzja sealing his win. This triumph marks a significant moment for Indian chess.

title: Can Supriya Life Sciences unlock its next leg of growth beyond APIs?
Israel-Iran war highlights: U.S. strikes Iran radar sites after Iranian drone launch
description: The U.S. military shot down ‌all of ​the Iranian drones, saying they “posed ⁠an immediate threat to ‌regional maritime traffic.”

description: Head coach Gambhir confirms that Sai Sudharsan will be given a long rope at No. 3; for the visitor, some of its leading white-ball stars, including ace leggie Rashid, are missing from the squad

title: Delhi Confidential: Future Tense
'Peddi' box office collection: Ram Charan's film witnesses sharp dip
description: Ram Charan's 'Peddi' experienced a significant box office drop on its second day, collecting Rs 26.90 crore net, a 47.3% decrease from its opening. Despite this, the film has amassed Rs 96.40 crore net domestically and Rs 150.49 crore worldwide. The Telugu market continues to be its strongest performer, with impressive occupancy rates.

title: ‘India acts in its own interest, pressure won’t work’: Putin defends ties with New Delhi
‘I don't care who we're leaving out': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi backed for India debut
description: Former England captain Nasser Hussain believes India should fast-track Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's international debut. The young batter's record-breaking IPL 2026 season has impressed many. Hussain feels Sooryavanshi's extraordinary performances make him impossible to ignore for the national team. His talent is undeniable and he is set for further challenges.

title: Govt re-appoints Swaminathan Janakiraman as RBI Deputy Governor for two years
West Bengal: TMC defection hands Congress civic body
description: Trinamool has lost control of the Mekhliganj municipality in Cooch Behar after its chairman defected to Congress, bringing six councillors with him. This marks the first civic body managed by Congress since the change of government in West Bengal. The chairman cited internal conflict within Trinamool as a reason for his move.

title: Won’t allow demographic change through influx: Amit Shah
description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced a pilot project for "smart borders" at multiple locations nationwide, aiming to bolster security and prevent demographic shifts through infiltration. This initiative, part of a quadrilateral security strategy, will integrate technology with local resources and security forces.  Shah emphasized the resolve to secure India's frontiers against threats like smuggling and trafficking.

title: Nepal’s Foreign Minister begins three-day India visit
'Gill will not come back': Ex-India batter makes big T20I captaincy claim
description: Former India batter Mohammad Kaif strongly backs Shreyas Iyer for the T20I captaincy, citing his IPL success with Gautam Gambhir and leading franchises to finals. Kaif believes Iyer's leadership and batting prowess make him the top choice, ahead of others like Jasprit Bumrah. He also suggested the team has moved past Hardik Pandya and Shubman Gill for the role.

title: 2 behind DU professor’s murder? Cops probe
description: Investigators are searching for two masked individuals seen leaving assistant professor Debosmita Paul's housing complex separately around the time of her suspected murder. Police are examining forensic evidence, delivery personnel, and domestic workers, while also addressing security lapses in the gated community.

title: Kerala High Court dismisses appeal against ED probe by firm linked to Pinarayi Vijayan’s daughter
This theatre festival in Bengaluru shines a spotlight on new voices in Kannada playwriting
description: Theatre festival Chiguru X Kusumaale will showcase plays developed through the Girish Karnad Fellowship for Kannada Playwriting. The themes range from the city in the ‘90s to examining the Koraga identity

title: CJP’s NEET stir today, Youth Congress rejects overture
description: Newly formed online group Cockroach Janta Party sought coordination with the Indian Youth Congress for its upcoming NEET protest, but the overture was rejected. The IYC stated it will not share platforms and is conducting its own nationwide protests, focusing on government accountability. Congress strategists remain skeptical of CJP's sudden emergence.

title: West Bengal CM Suvendu in Delhi to ‘discuss portfolios’
description: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari made a surprise visit to Delhi, meeting with BJP's Nitin Nabin and Sunil Bansal. Discussions reportedly focused on portfolio allocation and administrative workload distribution following the state cabinet expansion. The visit also aimed to accelerate the implementation of central government schemes in Bengal.

title: Facing desertion by MPs, Mamata Banerjee clips Abhishek’s wings
Cockroach Janta Party founder Dipke lands in Delhi today, police prepare for protest
Differential rates on deposits beyond RBI norms not acceptable, says Governor
Penguin pulls plug on Joe Sacco’s book on Muzaffarnagar riots
FIR filed against ‘Khan Sir’ in coaching centre firing case
1st tranche of US trade deal by mid-July: Piyush Goyal
description: India and the US are nearing the finalization of an interim trade agreement, with the first tranche expected to be executed by mid-July. Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal stated that negotiations are progressing rapidly to close all outstanding issues. A high-level American delegation is scheduled to visit India later this month.

title: Praggnanandhaa beats Keymer to win Norway Chess title
description: The 20-year-old player won four classical games on the trot to take the top spot after So’s chances of claiming the trophy ended after his game against Alireza Firouzja ended in a draw.

title: Oil India strikes natural gas in third Andaman offshore well
description: State-run Oil India Limited (OIL) has announced the discovery of natural gas in its third exploratory well, Vijayapuram-3, located off the Andaman Islands. This finding further strengthens the hydrocarbon potential of the region, with gas confirmed through continuous flaring and pressure build-up. OIL has now established hydrocarbon presence in two of its three wells in the Andaman offshore block.

title: Taylor Swift reportedly rejects Meghan Markle-style wedding spotlight buzz
description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly planning a deeply personal wedding, far from a celebrity-filled spectacle. Sources claim Swift does not want a Meghan Markle and Prince Harry-style event packed with famous guests for publicity. Instead, the couple allegedly wants only close friends and family present. Insiders said Swift “has no interest in turning her wedding into a power summit,” choosing meaningful relationships over Hollywood status and networking-driven invitations.

title: Great Nicobar Project isn't about defence: Rahul Gandhi
description: Congress MP Rahul Gandhi released a video of his Nicobar visit, calling the Great Nicobar Project a "lie" and alleging it benefits a businessman for hotels and casinos. He initiated an online petition, "we choose green over greed," urging Indians to protect the island's ecosystem and tribal communities from destruction.

title: No mass data leak: JEE (Advanced) authorities
description: JEE (Advanced) authorities have clarified that a cloud storage misconfiguration identified by an ethical hacker did not lead to any mass extraction of candidate data. IIT-Roorkee stated the issue, which occurred during technical interventions, was immediately rectified and access was restricted, with no impact on examination records or results.

title: Blood test can predict lung cancer 5 years before diagnosis
description: Scientists found blood markers that can predict lung cancer more than five years in advance. This breakthrough could help detect the disease early in India, where most cases are found late. A 14-protein signature in blood identified individuals at higher risk. This finding may lead to earlier monitoring and preventive measures for lung cancer.

title: Bangladesh border guards, BSF accuse each other of push-in attempts
description: Border Guard Bangladesh accused India's BSF of attempting to push over 70 individuals into Bangladesh near Lalmonirhat, Naogaon, and Chapainawabganj. Meanwhile, BSF reported thwarting a BGB attempt to help 10 Bangladeshis enter India. Both forces disowned stranded individuals, leading to heightened border patrols.

title: 'It is trite law': SC says documents linked to Official Secrets Act case needed for fair trial
description: The Supreme Court has ruled that an accused's right to a fair trial includes access to prosecution documents, even if classified under the Official Secrets Act. The court ordered the government to provide highly confidential documents to retired Major General V.K. Singh, facing charges for revealing secrets in his book, emphasizing fair trial rights.

title: GDP rose 7.7% in FY26, 7.8% in Q4; RBI trims this year's projection to 6.6%
description: India's economy surged by 7.8% in the Jan-March quarter, driven by strong investment, consistent farm output, and expansion in construction and tertiary sectors. This robust demand offset potential negative impacts from West Asian conflicts. The government remains committed to driving economic momentum through decisive policy measures.

title: From tax waivers to free hedges, RBI & govt join hands to boost Rupee
description: Government and RBI have launched new measures to attract foreign investment in bonds and bank deposits.  These initiatives aim to boost inflows by easing regulations and offering currency protection.  The rupee strengthened significantly following the announcements, with potential for substantial retail deposit growth.  Tax exemptions for foreign investors on government securities are also in place.

title: Spain thrash England 4-0, Germany qualify for Women's World Cup finals
description: England's ⁠hopes of securing a spot with a ⁠win in Mallorca were quickly snuffed out, with Patri Guijarro giving the Spaniards the lead in the 19th minute and Alexia Putellas making it ​2-0 before the break.

title: Pune court allows Sugandha Hiremath’s suit in Kalyani family dispute to continue
description: In two separate detailed orders, Joint Civil Judge J.G. Pawar refused to stay the proceedings as sought by Sugandha’s brother Gaurishankar Kalyani and his daughter, Sheetal Kalyani.

title: Serena Williams adds Berlin to her comeback tour and will play doubles
description: Berlin Open organizers said Williams will compete in doubles at the grass-court event starting on June 13. Her partner and the date of her first match will be announced later.

title: BJD criticised Centre for alleged delay in approval of Odisha Coastal Highway Project
description: Reacting to the approval of the Union Cabinet for the Coastal Highway between Rameshwar and Paradip connecting Konark, the BJD said ₹8301-crore coastal highway project was approved after long wait of 11 years.

title: Government ready to take more measures to insulate economy
description: The Indian government is preparing further policy steps to protect the economy from the West Asia conflict. These measures will ensure sufficient availability of goods and raw materials. The focus is on stabilizing the Indian currency and foreign exchange flows. Additional measures will be announced as needed. The government is closely monitoring trade deficits and potential impacts on subsidies.

title: G-Secs rally as govt and RBI ease foreign investing rules
description: Government and RBI measures to boost foreign investment in government securities led to a bond rally, with benchmark 10-year G-Sec yields softening. Exemptions from long-term capital gains and withholding taxes for FPIs in G-Secs, along with expanded investment options, are seen as positive for the fixed income market.

title: RBI cuts growth, hikes inflation forecast as West Asia crisis bites
description: The RBI maintained the repo rate at 5.25% amidst economic slowdown fears and rising inflation risks from the West Asia conflict.  Governor Sanjay Malhotra signaled concern over generalized inflation, cutting the FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% and raising inflation projections to 5.1%.

title: 12 hospitalised after ammonia leak in ice plant at Munambam in Kochi
description: Leak was brought under control promptly by fire and services personnel

title: Malviya Nagar blaze: Delhi CM reviews safety measures, directs sealing of all unsafe high-rises
Corrupt, negligent officials could face jail term, pension cuts: Sood
description: Officials found guilty of negligence or collusion to be held personally accountable in cases of loss of life or property: Minister; MCD Commissioner directed to seal all ongoing construction work beyond the permissible ground-plus-four floors, he adds

Mirra Andreeva's new calm meets surprise package Maja Chwalinska in French Open final
description: Facing the unheralded Lois Boisson for a spot in the French Open semifinals, the Russian teenager lost her temper, losing in straight sets after receiving a warning for angrily hitting a ball into the stands.

title: Waqf Board functioning without mandatory inclusion of non-Muslim members, says petition before HC
Police book ‘Khan Sir’ in firing case; govt to curb coaching rivalry
description: Khan’s bodyguards had fired in the air after his centre was attacked on June 2; Education Minister says State government will formulate a policy soon to curb rivalry among coaching institutes

title: Lottery fund fraud: accused says he burnt currency notes
Minister hails C-HED as an exemplary model, offers full support from govt.
description: The Centre for Heritage, Environment and Development receives the Bhoomimitra Award-2026, instituted by the Aluva Paristhithi Samrakshan Sangham, at a function held in Aluva on World Environment Day

title: Pre-monsoon works spared city of major waterlogging: Kochi Mayor
description: Locations prone to waterlogging remained largely unaffected, while minor flooding reported in a few pockets eased by afternoon, says Mayor

title: Kochi Corpn. to issue notice to CSML over streetlight maintenance
description: LED lights have drawn flak from councillors across political parties over their alleged erratic functioning and poor upkeep; CSML claims that at no point were more than 200 lights non-functional in a network of over 43,000 lights

title: Great Nicobar project meant to benefit Adani, it’s not about defence: Rahul Gandhi
description: On World Environment Day, the Opposition leader launches an online petition against the Great Nicobar Island transhipment hub project, urges youngsters to sign up and “choose green over greed”

title: PM Modi inspects indigenous defence systems at Surat facility, briefed about desi light tank Zorawar
description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited Larsen & Toubro's Armed Systems Complex in Surat, reviewing advanced defence equipment and indigenous armoured vehicles. He was briefed on 'Zorawar', India's first indigenous light battle tank designed for mountainous terrains and rapid deployment along the LAC.

title: Two killed, four injured in roof collapse at underground coal mine in Madhya Pradesh’s Shahdol
description: Workers were engaged in constructing a stopping wall when the incident occurred in the early morning hours, police said

title: After initial denial, CBSE invited ethical hacker to plug gaps in IT system
France brushes off Ivory Coast loss, calls it timely World Cup reminder
description: Didier Deschamps' side led through ⁠a superb first-half goal from Rayan Cherki but were overrun after the break as Guela Doue and Amad Diallo turned the game around ‌for the Elephants in Nantes.

title: Low magnesium levels: 3 signs that may indicate a deficiency

Kharge files nomination papers for Rajya Sabha polls from Karnataka

title: Supreme Court orders status quo on deportation of two women declared foreigners by Assam tribunal
Praggnanandhaa wins Norway Chess 2026 title after stunning final round victory
Neetu Kapoor calls Ranbir and Alia ‘outstanding’ parents to Raha: ‘Very scared of her mother’
Brayden McNabb's Stanley Cup final status in doubt after hospitalisation
description: Vegas Golden Knights defenseman Brayden McNabb's status is uncertain after a puck struck him in the face during Game 2 of the Stanley Cup Final. He traveled home with the team, but his availability for Game 3 remains unknown. McNabb, a key player for the Golden Knights, was injured by an 87-mph shot in the first period.

title: Hotel owner applied for licence renewal an hour after Malviya Nagar tragedy
description: An hour after a fire at Hauz Rani B&B, owner Lovkesh Bajaj applied to renew a ground-floor restaurant license that had expired. The Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) rejected the application, noting the establishment was operating in violation of norms. The incident highlights regulatory lapses and questions about enforcement.

title: Centre to deploy CoBRA units in Manipur before monsoon
description: Elite CRPF CoBRA commandos are being deployed to Manipur amidst escalating ethnic unrest, now involving the Naga community. This move follows a recent flashpoint that ignited fresh violence, leading to arson and abductions. Union Home Minister Amit Shah has ordered strong action against extremist elements across the state, aiming to restore order and public trust.

title: US adds 172,000 jobs in May, easing fears of labour market slowdown
Why the size of your prostate matters less than the daily symptoms

Meta's highest-paid employee’s 'health message' to Anthropic, OpenAI & Google
description: Meta's top AI executive, Alexandr Wang, revealed the company's strategy to challenge rivals like OpenAI and Google by focusing on health-related AI capabilities. While acknowledging current models aren't top-tier, Wang highlighted Meta's commitment to advancing AI for health applications, aiming to integrate these features into popular platforms like Facebook and Instagram.

title: Microsoft HR head Amy Coleman to employees: I want to be transparent about how things are feeling
description: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman shared employee survey results, highlighting increased employee "energized" and "empowered" feelings, alongside strengths in security and inclusion. However, challenges remain in opportunities for experience broadening, productivity support, and clarity on work's organizational connection. Coleman pledged greater transparency and communication amidst intense change.

title: Fortnite Shattered live event: Full start times and schedule for all regions
description: The Fortnite Shattered live event is almost here, bringing Chapter 7 Season 2 to its dramatic conclusion. Players can experience the event on June 5, with different story outcomes depending on whether they supported Team Foundation or Team Ice King. This one-time event will not be replayed, making it a must-watch for Fortnite fans. Check the full regional timings, learn when doors open, and find out how to join before matchmaking closes ahead of the transition to Chapter 7 Season 3.

title: 'Bro in a wheelchair?': Clavicular photos spark surgery speculation
description: Clavicular’s post-surgery photos have become a major talking point online. While the images clearly show him recovering from rhinoplasty, the leg braces and wheelchair seen in one photo led to widespread speculation about possible additional procedures. So far, no confirmed information has been released about any surgery other than the rhinoplasty, leaving many questions unanswered.
